strategies.caveats: Depreciation recapture must be recognized in year of sale regardless. Interest on deferred tax applies above $5M in installment obligations. Related party rules apply.

strategies.name: Historic Rehabilitation Tax Credit
strategies.description: 20% federal tax credit for qualified rehabilitation expenses on certified historic structures. Encourages preservation while providing significant tax benefits.
strategies.requirements: Certified historic structure (NPS listed or in historic district); substantial rehabilitation test (expenses exceed adjusted basis); NPS Part 1-3 approval
strategies.example: Rehabilitate a historic commercial building for $500K in qualified expenses. 20% credit = $100K, claimed over 5 years ($20K/year).
strategies.caveats: Credit claimed ratably over 5 years (not all at once). Must reduce depreciable basis by credit amount. Building must be depreciable. Recapture if disposed within 5 years.

strategies.name: Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC)
strategies.description: Federal tax credit for investors in qualified affordable housing projects. Credits are allocated by state housing agencies and provide dollar-for-dollar tax reductions over 10 years.
strategies.requirements: Investment in qualified LIHTC partnership/project; state housing agency allocation; 15-year compliance period; rent and income restrictions on tenants
strategies.example: Invest $200K in LIHTC syndication. Receive ~$180K in federal tax credits over 10 years plus depreciation deductions and potential residual value.
strategies.caveats: 15-year compliance period with strict rules. Recapture if noncompliance. Passive activity rules apply (need passive income or RE professional status). Complex partnership structures.

strategies.name: Timber & Natural Resource Depletion
strategies.description: Deduct the cost of timber, minerals, or other natural resources as they are harvested or extracted through depletion allowances. Timber can also qualify for capital gains treatment.
strategies.requirements: Ownership interest in timber, mineral, or natural resource property; established depletion basis; proper identification of timber volume or mineral reserves
strategies.example: Sell $100K of timber from 200 acres. With $30K depletion basis and §631(b) election, gain is taxed at long-term capital gains rate (15-20%) instead of ordinary rates.
strategies.caveats: Must establish depletion basis with professional timber cruise or mineral survey. Percentage depletion limited for certain minerals. Reforestation costs get amortization deduction.
strategies.irs_reference: IRC §611-§613; IRC §631; Form T (Timber); Schedule C or E

strategies.name: Oil & Gas Intangible Drilling Costs
strategies.description: Deduct intangible drilling costs (IDCs) — typically 65-85% of total well costs — in the year incurred. Working interest owners can also avoid passive activity limitations.
strategies.requirements: Working interest in oil or gas well; election to deduct IDCs currently (vs capitalize); AMT preference item consideration
strategies.example: Invest $100K in oil well drilling program. $75K in IDCs deducted immediately. At 37% bracket, saves $27,750 in year 1, plus ongoing depletion.
strategies.caveats: High economic risk (dry holes). IDCs are an AMT preference item. Working interest exception from passive rules requires unlimited liability. Percentage depletion has income limits.

strategies.name: Qualified Intermediary / Reverse 1031 Exchange
strategies.description: Use a reverse 1031 exchange to acquire replacement property before selling your relinquished property. An Exchange Accommodation Titleholder (EAT) holds the new property during the exchange period.
strategies.requirements: Qualified intermediary and EAT; 45-day identification + 180-day completion; both properties held for investment or business; cannot hold both simultaneously for more than 180 days
strategies.example: Find ideal replacement property before selling current rental. EAT acquires it for $800K. Sell old property 3 months later. Complete reverse 1031 exchange, deferring $150K in gains.
strategies.caveats: More expensive than forward exchange (EAT fees, carrying costs). Strict 180-day window. Additional financing complexity. Must follow Rev Proc 2000-37 safe harbor exactly.
